description: Format coverage matrix tracking which input capability classes doc-forge can convert, how well, and what representative test fixtures exist. Rows should capture materially different routing, provenance, or validation classes, not every combinatorial entry-surface permutation of the same supported capability. Agents query this to find untested formats and prioritize work.

statuses.untested: Format identified but no test fixtures or pipeline support
statuses.has-fixture: Test document exists but pipeline not yet built or passing
statuses.passing: Pipeline converts this format with acceptable accuracy
statuses.graduated: Converter migrated to Dossier — doc-forge no longer the primary owner

accuracy_dimensions.text_fidelity: Character-level accuracy of extracted text vs source (0.0-1.0)
accuracy_dimensions.structure_preservation: Tables, columns, headers, lists preserved correctly (0.0-1.0)
accuracy_dimensions.illustration_extraction: Illustrations detected, cropped, and cataloged correctly (0.0-1.0)
accuracy_dimensions.provenance_completeness: Every output traces to source page/location (0.0-1.0)

formats.description: Scanned PDF with primarily prose content

fixtures.notes: Repo-owned image-only prose PDF generated from checked-in source text. Story 167 adds maintained extractor-provenance smoke plus a fresh OCR proof whose normalized output matches the checked-in source text exactly.

Repo-owned image-only degraded/noisy synthetic prose PDF generated from the same checked-in `scanned-prose-mini.md` source via `generate_scanned_prose_fixture.py --preset degraded`. The current preset is intentionally visibly rougher than the clean slice: softer text, lower contrast, added scan noise, mild skew, and faint edge shadow. Story 210 re-proved the maintained generic PDF OCR lane on 2026-04-10 with page-level `ocr_quality` `0.94-0.96`, full `page_image_v1` provenance, and normalized text ratio `0.996038`.

scores.notes: Story 167 proved the clean repo-owned simple-prose scanned lane as an exact normalized match. Story 210 widened the bounded support slice to one repo-owned synthetic degraded/noisy variant on the same checked-in source text. After strengthening the degraded preset to be visibly rougher, the fresh `driver.py` proof on 2026-04-10 still preserved the prose with normalized text ratio `0.996038` and full provenance, while page-level `ocr_quality` fell to `0.94-0.96` and minor page-boundary section headings still degraded to paragraph tags on some pages. This remains synthetic evidence, not a blanket claim about real-world degraded scanned prose.

known_gaps: Current degraded/noisy evidence is still synthetic and simple-prose only; broader real-world degraded scanned-prose quality remains unmeasured.
Some section-transition headings still degrade to paragraph tags at page boundaries on the degraded synthetic fixture.

formats.description: Scanned PDF with dense tables (genealogy, reference books)

fixtures.notes: Shared local scanned PDF for the maintained Onward table-heavy lane. Matches the 127-page shared image-directory source.

scores.notes: Structure quality comes from Story 131 / the 2026-03-11 onward-table-fidelity refresh. Story 207 refreshed illustration quality against the maintained `image-crop-extraction` detector surface to `0.9703` on 2026-04-10, then rechecked the reviewed Onward publication seam from Stories 184 and 198 before promoting the canonical row. Story 157 restored maintained PDF-backed entry plus fresh provenance verification on the shared Onward PDF.

known_gaps: Handwritten annotations still weaken some table pages.

fixtures.notes: Same book as scanned-pdf-tables but ingested as pre-extracted images.

scores.notes: Shares the current scanned-image quality envelope: table structure at 0.95, illustration extraction now follows Story 207's fresh maintained `image-crop-extraction` rerun at `0.9703` on 2026-04-10, and provenance completeness remains 1.0 after Story 132 verification. Stories 184 and 198 still provide the reviewed Onward publication seam for this crop score.
Text passthrough — no OCR needed. Structure preservation N/A for plain text.
Markdown passthrough. Structure (headers, lists) preserved as-is.

known_gaps: No semantic structure extraction from markdown headers/lists
No tag stripping or semantic extraction from HTML structure

formats.description: PDFs with embedded text (no OCR needed), possibly with complex layouts

fixtures.notes: Supported. Repo-owned reviewed book-like fixture. Story 168 proved the bounded maintained Marker-lite native-text lane here, Story 170 reverified it against the OCR baseline, and Story 196 kept it inside the maintained four-case benchmark.

Supported. Repo-owned reproducible book-like handbook fixture generated from checked-in markdown via `testdata/generate_book_like_born_digital_fixture.py`. Story 196 added it to widen the maintained book-like proof surface beyond `tbotb-mini` and verified TOC-derived chapters with page-linked provenance.

Supported. Repo-owned reproducible flat born-digital fixture generated from checked-in source text. Story 171 proved the maintained non-TOC lane here with accepted `page_html_v1`, portion, bundle, and provenance artifacts; Story 196 kept it in the passing supported slice.

Supported. Repo-owned reproducible flat born-digital form-like fixture generated from checked-in source text. Story 177 widened the repeatable proof surface beyond the original prose-only mini fixture, and Story 196 kept it in the passing supported slice.

Comparison-only local flat born-digital PDF. Story 170 first exposed this short flat born-digital PDF as a downstream structure failure. Stories 171 and 196 reran it through the maintained non-TOC recipe and produced an accepted single-document bundle with page-linked provenance, but Story 196 keeps it outside the bounded passing gate because it is not repo-owned.

Comparison-only local form-heavy born-digital PDF. Story 170 first exposed this form-heavy born-digital PDF as a downstream structure failure. Stories 171 and 196 reran it through the maintained non-TOC recipe and produced an accepted single-document bundle with page-linked provenance, but Story 196 keeps it outside the bounded passing gate because it is not repo-owned.

known_gaps: Passing evidence is bounded to the Story 196 supported slice: two repo-owned book-like fixtures (`tbotb-mini`, `born-digital-handbook-mini`) plus two repo-owned flat/non-TOC fixtures (`flat-born-digital-mini`, `flat-born-digital-form-mini`).
The two shared local flat PDFs (`rfp`, `release-forms`) still rerun cleanly on the maintained non-TOC lane, but they are comparison-only evidence rather than part of the passing gate.

Story 177 absorbs bounded final-HTML heading polish on the proven slice: repeated short in-body h1/h2 labels are demoted and pathological long heading blocks are flattened into emphasis paragraphs, but `page_html_v1` still reflects raw Marker heading guesses before build-time cleanup.
Image-heavy or otherwise unproven born-digital PDFs can still honestly return `no-recipe-needed` when intake evidence does not justify the maintained book-like or non-TOC lanes.
Marker remains materially slower on cold start than the OCR baseline.

fixtures.notes: Repo-owned DOCX fixture generated from checked-in source JSON. Story 172 proved the first maintained lane; Story 175 re-verified it under the widened three-fixture proof surface.
Repo-owned DOCX fixture with simple multi-section prose that previously surfaced false chapter splits when Unstructured promoted sentence-like body text to top-level `Title`.

Repo-owned DOCX fixture with nested subheadings plus bullets. Story 175 used it to prove the widened maintained slice can keep nested heading content inside the parent chapter rather than inventing extra chapters.

known_gaps: Passing evidence is still bounded to the supported slice reviewed in Story 175: heading-based sections, prose, nested subheadings, simple bullet lists, and a simple table.
The maintained lane does not yet claim tracked changes, comments, text boxes, embedded charts, or other advanced Word features.
Pageless DOCX provenance currently relies on stable source element ids rather than page-linked open-original behavior.

fixtures.notes: Supported. Repo-owned XLSX workbook generated from checked-in source JSON. Story 175 proved the first maintained XLSX slice here; Story 193 rechecked it and kept the same simple table-sheet behavior with sheet-named entries and anchor-based provenance.

Supported. Repo-owned XLSX workbook with two simple table sheets (`Summary`, `Regions`). Story 193 used it to widen the maintained slice beyond `xlsx-mini` while keeping one HTML entry per supported sheet.
Supported. Repo-owned XLSX workbook with two separate table regions on one sheet. Story 193 verified that the maintained lane preserves both table blocks inside one sheet-named HTML entry.

Bounded unsupported probe. Story 193 reran it to mark the current boundary: Unstructured emitted `Budget Notes` and `Total` as heading blocks around the main table instead of preserving the merged-title / formula-summary structure as table content.

known_gaps: Passing evidence is still bounded to the verified simple-table slice from Story 193: table-only sheets, sheet-named entries, and multiple table regions on one sheet.
The maintained lane does not yet claim formulas, charts, images, comments, cell-level open-original behavior, or merged-title / formula-summary structures like `xlsx-merged-formula.xlsx`.
Workbook provenance currently relies on stable source element ids and sheet-named entries rather than cell-address anchors or explicit page-number fields.

fixtures.notes: Supported on the bounded maintained slice. Story 197 established the first PPTX direct-entry lane here: one title slide plus simple slide-title/list/prose slides, emitted as one HTML entry per slide with slide-number provenance.

known_gaps: Passing evidence is still bounded to the verified Story 197 probe slice: one title slide plus simple slide-title/list/prose slides emitted as one HTML entry per slide.
The maintained lane does not yet claim speaker notes, embedded media, charts, animations, or broader mixed-layout PowerPoint ownership.
PPTX provenance currently relies on stable source element ids plus slide-number anchors, not slide-object open-original behavior or richer layout anchoring.

fixtures.notes: Supported on the bounded maintained slice. Story 201 established the first EPUB direct-entry lane here: one repo-owned chapter-first prose fixture with package-title/package-author metadata, two emitted HTML chapters, and pageless provenance via source element ids only.

known_gaps: Passing evidence is still bounded to the verified Story 201 probe slice: chapter-first prose with one short list on the checked-in `epub-mini.epub` fixture.
The maintained lane does not yet claim image-only or scanned EPUBs, embedded media, footnotes, scripted content, complex navigation, or broader ebook-layout ownership.
EPUB provenance currently relies on stable source element ids plus package metadata; it does not claim printed page numbers, page-linked open-original behavior, or richer spine/item anchors.

formats.description: Plain-text email messages (.eml) with subject/from/to headers and one message body

fixtures.notes: Supported on the bounded maintained slice. Story 202 established the first `.eml` direct-entry lane on one repo-owned plain-text message with subject/from/to metadata, one pageless HTML entry, and provenance via `source_element_ids` only.

known_gaps: Passing evidence is still bounded to one checked-in plain-text `.eml` fixture with one simple message body and no attachments.
The maintained lane does not yet claim multipart HTML normalization, quoted-thread cleanup, attachment extraction, `.msg` parity, mailbox/thread ownership, or broader email automation support.

EML provenance currently relies on stable source element ids plus message metadata such as `subject`, `sent_from`, and `sent_to`; it does not claim printed page numbers, thread/message relationship graphs, or attachment-native anchors.

fixtures.notes: Supported on the bounded maintained slice. Story 203 established the first `.mbox` direct-entry lane on one repo-owned two-message plain-text archive with stdlib `mailbox.mbox` splitting, one pageless HTML entry per message in archive order, and provenance via `source_element_ids` only.

known_gaps: Passing evidence is still bounded to one checked-in plain-text `.mbox` fixture with two simple message bodies and no attachments.

The maintained lane does not yet claim quoted-thread cleanup, multipart HTML normalization, attachment extraction, `.msg` parity, broader mixed-archive ownership beyond the separate ZIP seam, mailbox connector workflows, or broader mailbox/thread ownership.

MBOX provenance currently relies on stable source element ids plus per-message metadata such as archive order, `subject`, `sent_from`, and `sent_to`; it does not claim printed page numbers, mailbox graph semantics, or attachment-native anchors.

formats.description: Checked HTML page snapshots (.html → structured content)

fixtures.notes: Supported on the bounded maintained slice. Story 200 established the first web-page direct-entry lane on a repo-owned checked HTML snapshot captured from `https://example.com/`: one `page_html_v1` row, one heading-derived chapter, and accepted `doc-web` bundle/provenance artifacts. This remains direct explicit-recipe entry only, not live URL automation.

known_gaps: Passing evidence is still bounded to one checked-in static HTML snapshot with clear heading/prose structure.
The maintained lane does not yet claim live URL fetch, JavaScript-rendered pages, multi-page crawling, authenticated pages, or generic boilerplate stripping across arbitrary websites.
Web-page provenance currently relies on the accepted `doc-web` block-anchor surface plus one synthetic source page anchor; it does not claim printed pages or browser-native open-original anchors.

formats.description: Photos of handwritten notes, letters, diaries

fixtures.notes: Repo-owned synthetic handwritten note image directory generated from checked-in transcript text. Story 179 proved the maintained generic image-directory OCR lane on this narrow highly legible slice.

Image-only PDF wrapper around the same synthetic handwritten pages. Story 179 verified `pypdf` extract-text lengths `[0, 0]` and matched the checked-in transcript exactly after normalization on the maintained generic PDF OCR lane.

Repo-owned faded synthetic handwritten note image directory generated from a checked-in transcript via `generate_handwritten_notes_fixture.py --preset faded`. Story 185 widened the maintained generic image-directory OCR lane to a third synthetic handwritten slice with smaller, lighter, more jittered text.

Image-only PDF wrapper around the faded synthetic handwritten pages. Story 185 verified `pypdf` extract-text lengths `[0, 0]` and matched the checked-in transcript exactly after normalization on the maintained generic PDF OCR lane.

Repo-owned rougher synthetic handwritten note image directory generated from a checked-in transcript via `generate_handwritten_notes_fixture.py --preset rough`. Story 182 widened the maintained generic image-directory OCR lane beyond the original legible slice while keeping the support claim explicitly synthetic-only.

Image-only PDF wrapper around the rough synthetic handwritten pages. Story 182 verified `pypdf` extract-text lengths `[0, 0, 0]` and matched the checked-in transcript exactly after normalization on the maintained generic PDF OCR lane.

Repo-owned real handwritten fixture derived from Library of Congress item `https://www.loc.gov/pictures/item/2023637825/`, using the checked-in `LC-DIG-ppmsca-86618` pages 2-3 spread. Story 186 proved maintained image-directory provenance on this real slice, but the generic OCR lane stayed only in the low-0.1 range on reruns. Story 189 added a bounded handwritten rescue recipe; the fresh Story 191 maintained-baseline rerun still only reached `0.895587` on image entry, and stronger direct-model probes in the same pass still stayed below bar, so Barney remains a real-slice OCR failure even after the rescue seam.

Image-only PDF wrapper around the same real LOC Barney spread. Story 186 verified `pypdf` extract-text lengths `[0]` and reran the maintained generic PDF OCR lane, which also stayed only in the low-0.1 range on the checked-in transcript excerpt. Story 189 added a bounded handwritten rescue recipe, but the fresh Story 191 maintained-baseline rerun only reached `0.756036` on PDF entry, which is still well below bar.

Second repo-owned real handwritten fixture derived from Library of Congress item `https://www.loc.gov/pictures/item/2023637835/`, using the checked-in LOC front page (`LC-DIG-ppmsca-86651`). Story 192 re-audited the source, trimmed the checked-in transcript to the exact visible front-page boundary, and reran the maintained handwritten rescue lane; the corrected image-entry score is now `0.677267`. That resolves the prior source/transcript mismatch for the repo-owned one-page fixture, but the OCR lane still misreads visible front-page text, so this remains a real-slice OCR failure rather than a hidden continuation-text blocker.

Image-only PDF wrapper around the same real LOC Alverson front page. Story 189 verified `pypdf` extract-text lengths `[0]`; Story 192 kept that one-page wrapper unchanged, corrected the transcript scope to the visible front page, and reran the maintained handwritten rescue lane to `0.681952` on PDF entry. The remaining gap is OCR fidelity on the visible page, not source/transcript misalignment inside the checked-in fixture.

known_gaps: The three repo-owned synthetic handwritten fixtures still pass exactly after normalization across six maintained handwritten-rescue image-directory and PDF OCR cases, but the fresh Story 192 five-fixture corpus rerun still falls to `pass_rate = 0.6` because Barney misses on both entry seams (`0.908567` image / `0.886568` pdf) and the corrected Alverson front-page fixture also remains below bar (`0.677267` image / `0.681952` pdf).

The bounded handwritten Gemini rescue seam is materially better than the historical generic low-0.1 Barney baseline, but it is still not stable or general enough: Barney continues to drift on names and phrases such as `Harlen`, `genesee`, and `father has had gone`, while corrected-scope Alverson still shows visible-page OCR misses such as the spurious leading `nothing`, `Battle of Chickamauga`, `lates novels`, and `excepatable`.

Story 192 resolved the truth-surface blocker for the repo-owned Alverson front-page fixture by trimming the transcript to the visible source boundary. The handwritten lane is now blocked by OCR capability on the current real fixtures, not by an internal source/transcript mismatch. A future two-page Alverson benchmark would need a separate back-page asset and transcript update instead of silently widening this fixture.
Messy cursive, degraded notes, and broader real-world handwriting collections remain unproven.

formats.description: Bounded ZIP archives or source-native folders routed by capability class: one generic direct-entry passthrough slice for already-supported member types, one PDF-member approved-handoff launch slice on ZIP and direct-folder entry, and one grouped image-member OCR-boundary slice on ZIP and direct-folder entry

fixtures.notes: Supported on the first bounded maintained mixed-archive slice. Story 205 established a ZIP-only lane on one repo-owned four-member fixture with nested DOCX, plain-text `.eml`, checked HTML snapshot, and one intentionally unsupported `.txt` member. The lane emits archive/member manifest rows, launches the supported members through existing maintained direct-entry recipes, and records an explicit blocked route row for the unsupported member instead of flattening the archive into one synthetic document.

Supported on the first bounded ZIP-only PDF-member approved-handoff launch continuation of the same mixed-input seam. Story 221 established the nested recommendation substrate on one repo-owned four-member ZIP with a flat born-digital PDF member, plain-text `.eml`, checked HTML snapshot, and one intentionally unsupported `.txt` member, and Story 223 extended that same fixture through a member-local approved-handoff launch into the maintained born-digital PDF recipe. The lane emits archive/member manifest and route rows, launches a nested recommendation intake run for the PDF member, records the emitted approved `intake_plan_v1` artifact as `first_downstream_artifact`, writes a member-local `intake_handoff_v1` sidecar, and launches the recommended born-digital PDF recipe while preserving the existing `.eml` / HTML launches and explicit blocked `.txt` row.

Supported on the first bounded ZIP-only grouped image-member OCR-boundary continuation of the same mixed-input seam. Story 224 established one repo-owned three-member ZIP with two page images under `pages/` plus one intentionally unsupported `.txt` member, launches exactly one grouped `--input-images` child run into the maintained image-directory recipe, records the emitted `01_images_dir_to_manifest_v1/pages_images_manifest.jsonl` artifact as `first_downstream_artifact`, reaches `02_ocr_ai_gpt51_v1/pages_html.jsonl` under the shared grouped child run id, and preserves the explicit blocked `.txt` row. The route rows keep shared `group_id`, `group_key`, `group_role`, `group_size`, `launch_input_path`, and `downstream_run_id` provenance across the grouped members.

Supported on the first bounded direct-folder continuation of the same mixed-input seam. Story 218 established one repo-owned four-member folder tree with nested DOCX, plain-text `.eml`, checked HTML snapshot, and one intentionally unsupported `.txt` member. The lane emits the same archive/member manifest and route schemas as the ZIP proof, but keeps `extracted_path` source-native instead of copying members into the run.

Supported on the first bounded direct-folder PDF-member approved-handoff launch continuation of the same mixed-input seam. Story 222 established the direct-folder recommendation substrate on one repo-owned four-member folder tree with a flat born-digital PDF member, plain-text `.eml`, checked HTML snapshot, and one intentionally unsupported `.txt` member, and Story 223 extends that same fixture through a member-local approved-handoff launch into the maintained born-digital PDF recipe. The lane emits source-native archive/member manifest and route rows, launches a nested intake run for the PDF member, records the emitted approved `intake_plan_v1` artifact as `first_downstream_artifact`, writes a member-local `intake_handoff_v1` sidecar, and launches the recommended born-digital PDF recipe while preserving the existing `.eml` / HTML launches and explicit blocked `.txt` row.

Supported on the first bounded direct-folder grouped image-member OCR-boundary continuation of the same mixed-input seam. Story 224 widened the grouped-image proof to one repo-owned three-member source-native folder tree with two page images under `pages/` plus one intentionally unsupported `.txt` member. The lane emits source-native archive/member manifest rows plus grouped member route rows with shared `group_id`, `group_key`, `group_role`, `group_size`, `launch_input_path`, `downstream_run_id`, and `first_downstream_artifact` fields, launches exactly one grouped `--input-images` child run into the maintained image-directory recipe from the source-native `pages/` directory, reaches `02_ocr_ai_gpt51_v1/pages_html.jsonl` under the shared grouped child run id, and preserves the explicit blocked `.txt` row.

known_gaps: Passing evidence is still bounded to six checked-in probes covering three behavior classes: direct-entry passthrough on one ZIP and one source-native folder mix, PDF-member approved-handoff launch on one ZIP and one source-native folder mix, and grouped image-member OCR-boundary continuation on one ZIP mix plus one source-native folder mix.

This row models routing/continuation classes rather than every `supported member type × container` permutation. Already-supported direct-entry member types belong to the generic passthrough class unless the container changes routing, provenance, downstream artifact shape, or validation boundaries.

The maintained lane does not yet claim scanned or handwritten PDF-member launch parity, grouped-image continuation beyond the first downstream `page_html_v1` artifact, nested archives, attachment extraction, ambiguous AI-assisted route decisions, or broad heterogeneous archive ownership.

Mixed-archive provenance currently relies on relative `member_path` / `member_id`, source-native or extracted member file paths depending on the entry surface, and downstream run ids or artifact paths; it does not flatten members into one combined bundle or claim archive-level page anchors.
